rulings.Case Title: ALMA PLACE VS. DANIEL PATTERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Daniel Patterson And Mary Pattersons Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTs DANIEL PATTERSON and MARY PATTERSON's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendant Pattersons' motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. Defendants fail to maintain their initial burden of production. Defendants fail to present evidence showing that the stairs and/or handrail did not constitute a dangerous condition of property. Even if the danger was "open and obvious," such a circumstance did not relieve defendants of their duty to remedy the condition. (See Osborn v. Mission Ready Mix (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 104, 119; Martinez v. Chippewa Enterprises, Inc. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1184.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/nl

rulings.Case Title: FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY DBA LINCOLN AUTOMOTIVE VS. ASFAW A TEDLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgement Pursuant To Ccp 473 (B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendants Motion, Declaration Of Andrew Quan; Exhibit A; [Proposed] Order; [Proposed] Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT ASFAW TEDLA Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgement Pursuant To Ccp 473 (B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendants Motion, Declaration Of Andrew Quan; Exhibit A; [Proposed] Order; [Proposed] Answer Defendant Asfaw Tedla's motion to set aside default and vacate default judgement pursuant to CCP § 473(b) is granted. Since any doubts as to the application of section 437 are resolved in favor of the party seeking relief, the court finds that Mr. Tesla has shown that the entry of default and default judgement were taken against him because of his or his counsel's mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect due to the understanding of Mr. Tedla's counsel's that entry of default will not be sought by plaintiff before notice is given to Mr. Tedla's counsel. (See Shapiro v. Clark (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 1128, 1139, (" Because the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, 'any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default'")). The default and default judgment shall be vacated and defendant shall file his proposed answer to the complaint within five days of the entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Tedla is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/nl

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB VS. RAYMOND H LUU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 27, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB's Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar in light of the judgment issued by Judge Haines on 12/17/18. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN ANN MASON ET AL VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 27, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs DAWN MASON and JAMES MASON's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to 1/24/19 @ 9 am in Dept 301. After reviewing the extensive briefing by both sides, the Court finds that the parties need to conduct a much more thorough meet and confer by telephone (not letter) to attempt to resolve the issues raised in the motion. Also, the parties are strongly encouraged to discuss a possible stipulated protective order if it is agreed one will be necessary in this case. The telephonic meet and confer shall occur by 1/11/19. No later than 1/17/19, both parties are directed to send an email to JPT McDonald at the email address in this tentative advising of the results of the meet and confer (with specific references to the RFPDS at issue) and the matters remaining to be addressed by the Court at the hearing on 1/24/19. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROSS H. MOORE VS. UNICORN CLUB, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 27, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT UNICORN CLUB, LTD. Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint Defendant Unicorn Club, LTD.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is GRANTED. The interest of justice is served and leave is properly granted under CCP section 428.50(c) where a proposed cross-complaint is based on facts put in issue by the complaint and answer. (Danzinger v. Peebler (1948) 88 Cal.App.2d 307, 310.) Plaintiff Ross Moore has not demonstrated any prejudice in granting leave to Defendant to file the cross-complaint. This motion was made only eight months after the complaint was filed, six months after answer filed, and only weeks after obtaining the documents and testimony needed by Defendant to support its claims. Moreover, the trial date is still several months away. Glogau v. Hagan (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 313 does not stand for the proposition that the court should not grant leave to file any cross-complaint that might be subject to a demurrer. Rather, the case looked to various factors, including an 11 month delay from time of answer to complaint, the existence of a prior action based upon causes alleged in the cross-complaint that was still pending, as well as the fact that the claims were demurrable. The merits of the claims in the cross-complaint cannot be litigated until the cross-complaint is filed and the parties have satisfied their pre-demurrer meet and confer obligations under CCP section 430.41. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Unicorn Club is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/nl
